title: 10 best herbal essential oils for nausea
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for nausea are a natural remedy that have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and digestive discomfort. These oils work by targeting the nervous system and calming the stomach, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with nausea include peppermint, ginger, and fennel, which can be inhaled directly or added to a carrier oil for massage. By using these oils, individuals can find relief from morning sickness, motion sickness, and post-operative nausea, improving their quality of life and reducing stress and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an upset stomach and calm digestive discomfort. The oil's active compound, gingerol, has been shown to bind to serotonin receptors in the gut, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting. Additionally, ginger's warming and stimulating effects can help to improve digestion and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, morning sickness, and chemotherapy-induced nausea.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger essential oil stimulates digestive enzymes and relaxes the muscles in the stomach, helping to alleviate nausea by improving digestion and reducing inflammation.
Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in ginger essential oil help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to feelings of nausea and vomiting.
Vomiting Relief Agent: Ginger essential oil has been shown to have a direct effect on the vagus nerve, which regulates stomach contractions and relaxes muscles to relieve vomiting and nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of ginger contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea.
Buds: Fresh ginger buds are rich in volatile oils that can be extracted to create a gentle, yet effective essential oil for relieving nausea and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots from your garden or local market weigh 200g.
Clean the ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water scrubbing off dirt and debris.
Chop the clean ginger roots into small pieces weighing 150g.
Combine the chopped ginger with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking daily until the oil has turned golden yellow.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with nausea because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate digestive discomfort. When inhaled, the aroma of peppermint oil stimulates the brain's vagus nerve, which can help slow down digestion and reduce stomach upset. Additionally, peppermint oil can also help relax the muscles in the gut, reducing spasms and cramping that often contribute to nausea. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from queasiness, making it a popular choice for those prone to motion sickness or morning sickness during pregnancy.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil's menthol content stimulates digestive enzymes, helping to break down food and alleviate symptoms of nausea and indigestion.
Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint essential oil help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to feelings of nausea and vomiting.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil's antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to relieve cramping, spasms, and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are used to create a calming and soothing oil that helps alleviate nausea due to their high concentration of menthol and other therapeutic compounds.
Buds: The buds of the peppermint plant contain concentrated amounts of volatile oils, which are released when steam-distilled to create an oil that effectively relieves nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a single layer on paper towels or wire racks for 24-48 hours.
Place one cup of dried peppermint leaves in a clean glass jar and cover with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate oil extraction from the peppermint leaves.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids, then store the mentha x piperita essential oil in an amber glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with nausea because its calming and soothing properties can help to alleviate feelings of discomfort and unease associated with nausea. The oil's sweet, floral aroma can also help to ease digestive issues by promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones that can exacerbate nausea. Additionally, the antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties in lavender oil may help to calm stomach muscles and reduce inflammation, further alleviating nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative: English Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help alleviate nausea caused by stress, anxiety, or digestive issues.
Digestive Soothing: The oils' anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe digestive tract irritations and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with conditions like IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome).

Vomiting-Inhibiting Compounds: English Lavender essential oil contains compounds like linalool, which have been shown to inhibit vomiting and alleviate nausea symptoms by calming the stomach and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain compounds like linalyl acetate, which helps to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Buds: The buds of English lavender are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh lavender flowers and leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested lavender material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours to preserve enzymes.
Combine one part dried lavender material with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, allowing the essential oils to infuse slowly.
Strain and decant the infused oil through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, discarding solids and storing in cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to relax the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to ease spasms and cramps associated with nausea, while its gentle sedative effects can promote a sense of calm and tranquility, helping to alleviate feelings of queasiness and discomfort. This makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of morning sickness during pregnancy and chemotherapy-induced nausea.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Chamomile essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and alleviate nausea symptoms.
Antispasmodic Properties: Chamomile oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to nausea, heartburn, and other stomach-related issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties that can help alleviate nausea symptoms.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making essential oils due to their high concentration of apigenin, an flavonoid that has been proven to have anti-emetic effects and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn on a dry day when dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or using desiccants like silica gel.
Combine one part dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 hours or overnight then filter the oil using cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Store the filtered oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight at room temperature for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to ease stomach discomfort and reduce symptoms of nausea. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds work to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, sage's sweet, earthy aroma can help to calm the mind and body, further reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and gut, which can contribute to nausea and other digestive issues.
Carminative properties: Sage essential oil has carminative properties, meaning it helps to release gas from the digestive tract and alleviate cramps and spasms that can exacerbate nausea.
Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain compounds that can help soothe stomach discomfort and reduce nausea, making them a popular choice for natural remedies.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate nausea symptoms and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves for distillation within one week of harvesting.
Weigh and mix 50 grams of dried salvia sclarea flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass container.
Place the mixture in a double boiler setup and heat it gently at 60°c for two hours to facilitate solvent extraction.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for eight hours allowing the essential oil to separate from the plant material.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural properties that aid in digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort. The oil's carminative and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe the digestive system, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, fennel's ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines also helps to calm nausea and vomiting. When inhaled or applied topically, fennel essential oil can provide rapid relief from nausea and related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea caused by irritation or discomfort.
Digestive stimulant: Fennel oil stimulates digestive enzymes and improves digestion, which can help relieve nausea caused by poor digestion or sluggish metabolism.
Carminative: Fennel oil's carminative property helps release gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate nausea caused by indigestion or food intolerances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in relieving nausea and vomiting.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds in mid-summer when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested seeds in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds using 10 grams of seed per 100 milliliters of water.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil after 1 hour of distillation to remove impurities.
Store the final foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil has a gentle, warm aroma that can ease digestive discomforts, reducing feelings of queasiness and unease. Additionally, dill's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax the stomach muscles, alleviating cramps and spasms that often accompany nausea. As a result, inhaling or applying dill essential oil topically can provide quick relief from morning sickness, motion sickness, and other forms of nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dill essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carvone and limonene, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in dill essential oil, including eugenol and beta-carotene, help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nausea.

Carminative and digestive-stimulating properties: Dill essential oil's volatile oils, such as limonene and cineole, have natural carminative and digestive-stimulating effects, helping to ease digestion, relieve flatulence, and reduce feelings of nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dill plant contain compounds like carvone and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate nausea symptoms.
Seeds: Dill seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils, including carvone and limonene, which are known for their calming effects and ability to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh anethum graveolens leaves and stems from your garden or store-bought organic supply.
Cleanly wash the harvested material in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned material into small pieces to facilitate steam distillation process within 30 minutes.
Steam distill the chopped material using a commercial-grade distillation apparatus for 2 hours at atmospheric pressure.
Filter and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids within 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to alleviate stomach upset and reduce feelings of queasiness. The citrusy scent of lemongrass has been shown to ease digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, by stimulating the digestion process and promoting relaxation. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, further contributing to its nausea-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Cytotoxins: Lemongrass essential oil contains compounds that have cytotoxic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea symptoms.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in lemongrass essential oil help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative damage in the body, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the digestive system.

Carminative properties: Lemongrass essential oil has a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to relax spasms and reduce flatulence, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by digestive discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Buds: Lemongrass buds are a rich source of citral, a natural compound with sedative and anti-nausea effects, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for nausea relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning for maximum potency and quality.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or air dry them naturally slowly.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still with distilled water at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours carefully.
Filter and bottle the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with nausea because of its potent anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The oil contains limonene, a natural compound that has been shown to ease digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When inhaled or applied topically, bitter orange oil can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate nausea symptoms by reducing spasms and cramps in the digestive tract. Additionally, its calming properties can also help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of nausea, promoting overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Bitter orange essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which means it can help relax muscles and relieve spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea caused by digestive issues.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of bitter orange essential oil may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea, making it a useful remedy for alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other inflammatory conditions.

Uterotropic: Bitter orange essential oil has uterotropic properties, meaning it can stimulate the uterus and improve digestion, which can help alleviate nausea caused by hormonal imbalances or menstruation-related digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of limonene, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits, making them effective in reducing nausea.
Seeds: Bitter orange seeds are rich in citral, a natural antispasmodic that helps to relax the stomach muscles, providing relief from nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus x aurantium peels and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the peels in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 24 hours or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Combine the dried peels with carrier oil such as jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:5 and let it steep for 4 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard solids after 30 minutes.
Store the citrus x aurantium essential oil in an amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ylang-ylang essential oils helps with nausea because of its unique properties that stimulate the brain's limbic system, which is responsible for regulating emotions and mood. The oil's sweet, floral scent also has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-related symptoms that often contribute to nausea. Additionally, ylang-ylang oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness, pregnancy, and other conditions.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Ylang-ylang oil helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea.
Anti-anxiety: The calming and soothing effects of ylang-ylang oil can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for nausea.

Digestive stimulant: Ylang-ylang oil has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea caused by slowed or blocked digestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the flowers are known for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving nausea symptoms.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, the seeds have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-nausea properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cananga odorata, approximately 100 grams for every 10 milliliters of final product.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and gentle soap to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the washed leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves at atmospheric pressure using a clevenger apparatus, collecting 10 milliliters of essential oil after 2 hours of processing.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nausea with herbal essential oils is to dilute a few drops of peppermint, ginger, or lemon balm oil in a carrier oil and apply topically to the wrists, temples, or behind the ears. You can also inhale these oils directly from a cloth or handkerchief, taking slow, deep breaths to help soothe the stomach. Additionally, add a few drops of these oils to your bath water for a relaxing and calming experience.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive disorders such as bloating, cramps, and indigestion. Peppermint oil, for example, can help alleviate symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome) by reducing inflammation and spasms in the intestines. Ginger oil is another natural remedy used to treat nausea-related issues like motion sickness and morning sickness during pregnancy.
